extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose antecedent is a conjunction that includes low_ejection_fraction (along with hypertension, hepatic_impairment, pregnancy, renal_impairment, hyperkalemia, peptic_ulcer, heart_failure). rec_drug_b is set true by rule rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50) whenever low_ejection_fraction is true. Because low_ejection_fraction is one of the conjuncts required for rec_drug_a, ANY input that fires rec_drug_a necessarily also fires rec_drug_b. The two rules write to different outputs (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b), so the per-output priority/tie-breaking conflict resolution does not prevent both from firing — each wins on its own output. Defaults are irrelevant here since both outputs are actively set. Concrete counterexample: set all eight conjuncts (low_ejection_fraction, hypertension, hepatic_impairment, pregnancy, renal_impairment, hyperkalemia, peptic_ulcer, heart_failure) to true; qt_prolongation and elderly only affect rec_drug_c and are set false. Then rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a = true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b = true), so rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b is true, violating mutual exclusion.
